Guide

How to Choose Roofing Software (2026)

Roofing software has more variety than any other trade. Here's how to find the right fit for your operation.

✓ Updated April 2026

Roofing companies face a more complex software landscape than most trades. Between insurance restoration workflows, aerial measurement integrations, storm-chaser CRM needs, and retail replacement project management, no single platform serves every roofing business equally well. This guide helps you figure out what you actually need before buying anything.

Step 1: Identify your primary business model

Before evaluating any software, answer this question: what percentage of your revenue comes from insurance restoration vs. retail replacement vs. new construction roofing?

Primarily insurance restoration (50%+ of revenue)

You need a platform built around the insurance claim cycle. The workflow that matters: lead intake → inspection → adjuster meeting → supplement tracking → job scheduling → material delivery → installation → final invoice. Look for insurance-specific CRM features, supplement documentation, and EagleView/aerial measurement integration.

Best fit: JobNimbus or AccuLynx

Primarily retail replacement

You're bidding jobs competitively, generating professional proposals, scheduling crews, and collecting payment. The insurance claim workflow is largely irrelevant. What matters is speed from lead to signed contract, crew scheduling across multiple jobs, and invoicing.

Best fit: Jobber, Contractor Foreman, or JobTread

Primarily new construction roofing (sub to builders)

You're a sub on residential and commercial new builds. You need bid management, scheduling coordination with GCs, crew time tracking, and invoicing. You may get plan access through the GC's platform (Buildertrend, Procore) for free.

Best fit: Contractor Foreman or Jobber for your internal management; access the GC's platform through their invitation

Step 2: Assess your scale and team

Solo or 1–2 person operation

Software investment should be minimal. Jobber at $69/mo or Contractor Foreman at $49/mo gives you professional proposals and invoicing without overpaying. Focus on the tools that win jobs (professional proposals) and get paid (online invoicing).

5–20 job crews, 2–5 office staff

This is where purpose-built roofing software starts delivering ROI. At this scale, you're managing lead pipelines, crew scheduling across multiple jobs, and supplement negotiations simultaneously. JobNimbus at this scale handles the complexity that generic tools can't.

20+ jobs per week, dedicated sales team

AccuLynx's production board, direct material ordering, and subcontractor management become valuable at this volume. The cost of a disorganized job at this scale (wrong material delivered, crew shows up on wrong day) exceeds the software cost many times over.

Step 3: Evaluate the features that matter for roofing

Aerial measurement integration

EagleView and Hover are the dominant aerial measurement services. If you're pricing roofs without physically measuring them (storm chasing, large territory, high volume), integration with one of these services saves significant time per estimate. JobNimbus and AccuLynx integrate natively; for platforms without native integration, EagleView offers standalone reports you can import manually.

Material ordering workflow

Can you order materials from your distributor directly from the platform, tied to a specific job? AccuLynx supports this. For high-volume roofing companies, this eliminates phone calls and manual POs for each job's material order.

Storm damage documentation

For insurance claims, photo documentation of damage is everything. Mobile apps that let your field rep photograph damage, annotate photos, and attach them directly to the claim are essential. JobNimbus's mobile app does this well.

Customer communication automation

Roofing has longer sales cycles than most service work. Automated follow-up sequences (email/text) keep leads warm between the initial inspection and the signed contract. Jobber's client hub and JobNimbus's automation features handle this.

Top roofing software at each price point

Budget Best Option Why
Under $100/mo Contractor Foreman ($49) Full feature set, minimal cost
$100–$200/mo Jobber ($69–$169) Clean UX, fast quotes, payments
$200–$400/mo JobNimbus (~$200) Insurance workflow, aerial measurement
$400+/mo AccuLynx (custom) Material ordering, production management

Step 4: Evaluate your accounting integration needs

Whatever roofing software you choose, it needs to connect to your accounting software. QuickBooks is the standard; most roofing platforms integrate with both QuickBooks Online and Desktop. Before signing up, verify the integration works for your version and that the sync handles invoices, payments, and expenses in both directions.

Step 5: Questions to ask vendors before buying

  • Does your platform have native EagleView or Hover integration? What does that cost?
  • How does the insurance supplement workflow work specifically?
  • What QuickBooks version does your integration support?
  • How many users are included at the base price? What's the per-user cost?
  • What does onboarding look like — is there a dedicated implementation specialist?
  • What's your cancellation policy and data export process?

Red flags to watch for

  • No public pricing: If a roofing software company won't tell you the price without a sales call, that's a signal about what the relationship will look like afterward.
  • Aerial measurement as an extra charge: Some platforms charge separately for each EagleView report pulled through their integration. Understand the per-report cost before assuming it's included.
  • Per-user pricing that isn't obvious: "Starts at $99/mo" sometimes means $99/mo for one user; a team of 5 might be $495. Ask about the realistic cost for your actual team size.

Not sure which platform fits your roofing business? If you do any residential work — roofing + exterior, roofing + siding, or light GC work — Buildertrend gives you a complete residential contractor platform beyond just roofing.

Visit Buildertrend →

The honest bottom line

For pure roofing companies doing insurance restoration: JobNimbus or AccuLynx are purpose-built and worth their cost at any meaningful volume. For retail-focused roofers or small operations: Jobber or Contractor Foreman deliver enough at significantly lower prices. Don't let a roofing-specific platform's marketing persuade you to pay for insurance workflow features you don't use.
